title: UPI OC181 FY 23 24 Compliance to Merchant Onboarding in UPI and Usage Limits
text:
NEED

NATIONAL PAYMENTS CORPORATION OF INDIA

NPCVUPIIOC181/2023-24 16" November, 2023

To,
All Member Banks of Unified Payments Interface (UP!)

Dear Madan/Sir,
Subject: Compliance to Merchant Onboarding in UPI and Usage limits

Vide reference to NPCI/UPI/OC No.70/2019-20 and NPCI/UPI/OC No 72/2019-20, NPCI had
introduced P2PM category of merchant with an intent of bringing small merchant or vendors with

low value ticket size into the digital framework.

As per UPI guidelines, the obligation to acquire, manage and monitor merchant is the
responsibility of acquiring member banks and they shall take full liability for the merchants on-
boarded by them either directly or through aggregator/partners.

In order to align the UPI usage to the appropriate category of P2P, P2PM and P2M the following
guidelines are being issued to the eco-system:

4. In continuation to NPCI circular NPCI/UPVOC No 141/2021-22 dated 24" March, 2022
NPCI has defined the limit for user to receive funds in their bank account via P2P in 24
hours. With reference the point of circular, the limit is further revised to

a. 25 successful transactions AND
b. & 4.0 lakh cumulative credit limit

The mentioned change is to be implemented by 10% December 2023.

2. As per the NPCI guidelines, MCC 7407 has been assigned for P2PM category of
merchant. Acquirers shall populate the correct MCC in the ‘subCode’ field of the
merchant tag in UPI, use to identify the nature of merchant business. Acquirers are
required to make the necessary changes for the same by 15'" February, 2024.

3. Itis re-iterated that Merchant shall be on-boarded by the acquirer only under the category
of P2M/P2PM and are hereby instructed that any wrong caiegorization of merchant
under the category of P2P has to be corrected and tagged properly under P2M/P2PM
as per the NPCI guidelines. The correct categorization needs to be completed by 10"
December, 2023.

JOOIA, The Capital, B Wing, 10% Floor,

Bandra Kurla Complex, Bandra (E), Mumbai 400 O51-
T: +91 22 40009100 F: +91 22 40009101
contact@npci.org.in www.npci.org.in

CIN: U74990MH2008NPL 189067


Nee)

NATIONAL PAYMENTS CORPORATION OF INDIA

it is also re-iterated that acquirers shall take the necessary due diligence and ensure hygiene
as mentioned in the NPCI/UPI/Rupay/OC-118/2021-22 dated 8" September, 2021 for

onboarding of merchants.

Yours sincerely,

SD/-
Kuna! Kalawatia
Chief of Products
